title: ilex dubia var. macropoda
intro: Ilex dubia var. macropoda is a lesser-known variety of the Ilex genus, commonly referred to as the "holy wood" or "holy tree" due to its association with sacred and medicinal properties in traditional medicine. The plant has been found to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, making it a valuable resource in the treatment of various health conditions. Ilex dubia var. macropoda has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of fever, cold, and cough, as well as to treat digestive issues and skin problems. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
